task_completion.description: The document contains the correct title and course content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'ENVS 410: Urban Ecology and Sustainability — Fall 2024 Syllabus' at the top of the document in OpenOffice Writer. Content should include references to Dr. Samira Al-Rashid or Urban Ecology.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is included at the beginning.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a structured Table of Contents block (often with a gray background or dotted leader lines) at the start of the document, listing sections like Course Information, Course Description, etc.

task_completion.description: Page numbers are present in the footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom margin (footer area) of the document pages for visible page numbers.

task_completion.description: Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles are applied to the appropriate sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for visually distinct, larger/bold text for main sections (e.g., Course Information, Course Schedule) and slightly smaller bold text for subsections under Course/University Policies. The Styles dropdown in the toolbar or the Navigator panel may explicitly show 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2'.

task_completion.description: A Weekly Schedule table and a grading breakdown table are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for two grid-based tables in the document: a large Weekly Schedule table with columns for Week, Date, Topic, Reading, Assignment (containing around 16 rows), and a smaller grading breakdown table.

task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the OpenOffice Writer window title bar for 'ENVS410_Syllabus_Fall2024.odt' or check a file manager/terminal showing this file in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenOffice Writer GUI to create and format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the OpenOffice Writer interface being actively used, rather than just terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: The tables are actual word processor tables, not just formatted text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for table gridlines, table formatting toolbars, or table properties dialogs, ensuring the data is in actual table cells rather than just separated by tabs or spaces.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the course data from the provided JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the course_data.json file open in a text editor, terminal, or a script being used to read it, and ensure the document contains specific data from it (e.g., Forman, Beatley, Portland State University).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The syllabus is for ENVS 410, taught by Dr. Samira Al-Rashid. Required content includes references to 'urban ecology', 'Portland State University', and textbook authors 'Forman' and 'Beatley'. The document must have at least 8 Heading 1 sections, 5 Heading 2 subsections, and a 16-row weekly schedule table. The final file must be saved as ENVS410_Syllabus_Fall2024.odt in /home/ga/Documents/.
